sections.title: Featured Collection
sections: A selection of works that highlight key themes and projects within this branch. These pieces represent both personal milestones and defining creative moments across recent releases.

items.title: The Unreachable Flower
about: A reflection on longing for something meaningful that may always remain out of reach.
description: This song explores the emotional tension between hope and acceptance. It focuses on pursuing something beautiful or meaningful while recognizing that it may never fully be attained.

items.title: Decade Search
about: A narrative song based on journal entries documenting a long search for a childhood friend.
description: In 2015 I began searching for a childhood friend I had lost contact with. Over the years I wrote journal entries documenting the search and the thoughts that came with it. This song adapts those entries into a condensed narrative, shaping years of reflection into a single story-driven track.

items.title: Free To Be Me
about: Stepping into who I am without apology.
description: This track closes on identity, freedom, and self-acceptance. After everything before it, this song represents reaching a place where I can stand as myself more fully, without hiding, bending, or being defined only by what happened to me.

items.title: Already In Changing Motion
about: A reflection on a year filled with overlapping life transitions.
description: This song was written about a period where multiple major life changes were happening at once. School, work, volunteering, business projects, and searching for a home all began moving forward simultaneously, creating the feeling of constant momentum.

items.title: The Parts You Don't See
about: A personal reflection on the deeper aspects of identity.
description: People often ask how to describe yourself, but most introductions only capture surface details. This song explores the deeper motivations, experiences, and perspectives that shape who I really am.

items.title: Under the Tree
about: A song tied to my dad's death.
description: One of the most directly personal tracks on the album, this song centers on the loss of my dad. It marks a defining moment in my life and carries the weight, memory, and lasting emotional impact of that event.

sections.title: ZOLLZIS P ZIE
sections.about: A digital album built around identity, mindset, and the internal code that defines how I think, act, and move through life. Each track reflects a core part of who I am, from logic and contradiction to drive, persistence, and freedom.
body: ZOLLZIS P ZIE is an identity-driven album built from the systems, beliefs, and patterns that shape me.
Each song represents a different layer of who I am, from how I think and process the world to how I act and push forward.
The tracks move through structure, contradiction, accountability, willpower, persistence, and constant motion.
It starts with foundation and self-definition, builds through pressure and drive, and ends in movement and freedom as the result of it all.

sections.title: ZIDBZLIZEZDD
sections.about: A digital album built around the defining moments, shifts, losses, struggles, and identity changes that shaped my life. Each track reflects a time, state of mind, or turning point that stood out as significant in my story.
body: ZIDBZLIZEZDD is a story-driven album built from different parts of my life.
Each song represents a meaningful period, emotional state, or defining event that helped shape who I became.
The tracks move through memory, struggle, pressure, loss, identity, and growth.
The opening section stands apart slightly, acting as a collection of early memories shared by family that I turned into a song, rather than one single sharply defined event.

dropdowns.title: What They Remember of Me
dropdowns.about: A digital album built from early memories told through the voices of my family, capturing moments from 2000 to 2010 that helped shape the foundation of who I am.
dropdowns.description: What They Remember of Me is a memory-driven collection created from stories shared by my grandmothers, my mom, and my Aunt Bobbie. Each track reflects a moment, perspective, or piece of my early life as seen through the people who were there. Instead of a single narrative, the album is built from different viewpoints, blending together to form a picture of my beginnings. It captures small moments, meaningful memories, and the impressions I left before I was old enough to fully understand them myself.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1 (reframed)
They tell me I was dancing in the living room, no rhythm, didn’t care
Pebbles and Bam-Bam, costumes everywhere
Me and my cousins—lion, scarecrow, tin man laughs
Somewhere in the middle just trying to make it last
I’ve heard about those Christmas lights, how small I used to seem
Hiding by my mama when the room got loud on me
They say I ran back just to tell her every story in my head
Said “I’m gonna live with you forever” like I meant what I said
Chorus (slight shift — keep strong core)
They remember me different than I remember myself
In the moments I forgot, they were watching it all
Every laugh, every mess, every story I’d tell
I was growing into someone they could already tell
Through their eyes, I was brighter than I knew
Through their eyes, every little thing was me
Time moves on but it don’t erase
The way they see me… through those days
Verse 2 (reframed)
They say I shot a rocket, watched it disappear
Swore it hit the stars somewhere past here
They tell me I built trains running all through the house
Every room a world I was figuring out
I’ve heard I climbed the fridge with an apple and a blade
Too young to know the danger, just a choice I made
Cut a watermelon like I had it all planned
Didn’t ask permission — I just did what I can
Chorus (slight shift — keep strong core)
They remember me different than I remember myself
In the moments I forgot, they were watching it all
Every laugh, every mess, every story I’d tell
I was growing into someone they could already tell
Through their eyes, I was brighter than I knew
Through their eyes, every little thing was me
Time moves on but it don’t erase
The way they see me… through those days
Verse 3 (this one hits hard with framing)
They tell me I was precious, first one from the start
Little coat and cap, stealing every heart
I’ve heard they broke the rules, gave me candy anyway
Said my eyes lit up — made the guilt just fade
They say I rode the Harley, laughing in the yard
Didn’t matter how small — I went that hard
Sat on grandma’s lap even when I’d outgrown
Still gave her that moment like it was her own
Chorus (slight shift — keep strong core)
They remember me different than I remember myself
In the moments I forgot, they were watching it all
Every laugh, every mess, every story I’d tell
I was growing into someone they could already tell
Through their eyes, I was brighter than I knew
Through their eyes, every little thing was me
Time moves on but it don’t erase
The way they see me… through those days
Verse 4 (same idea)
They tell me I got lost, didn’t know the way back
Cops had to find me — yeah, imagine that
Paper airplanes flying with the neighbor next door
Pepper in the wind like I’d done it before
I’ve heard about my first tooth, chaos in between
Cousins and those moments I don’t really see
Running through the house like the world was mine
All the things I lived… but left behind
Bridge (this becomes your anchor — very important)
These ain’t my memories — but they live in me
From the ones who were there, from the ones who could see
My grandmas, my mom, and Aunt Bobbie too
Every story you gave… I rebuilt into truth
Final Chorus (small tweak for closure)
They remember me different than I remember myself
Every little moment, even when I felt small
I was never just passing through time and space
I was building a life they still can’t replace
Through their eyes, I was more than I knew
Through their eyes, every moment was me
And even now as the years erase
I still see me… through those days

dropdowns.title: Too Far Gone
dropdowns.about: a song about the monster inside taking over.
dropdowns.description: A track centered on losing control to something darker inside. It reflects a period where inner struggle stopped feeling distant and started taking over in a way that felt real, personal, and dangerous.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: (Verse 1)
I’m not coming back, No… I can’t come back, I’m too far gone
I can feel my hands move, But they don’t feel like mine
Every word I say, Feels like a lie
I know what’s coming next, already knowing it’s crossing a line
The pain I feel, is too unbearable to heal
I’m stuck here, not able to change what’s real, forced to watch this hell
(Chorus)
I’m not coming back, No… I can’t come back, I’m too far gone
The monster inside of me its taking over slowly
I’m still here, I can see, But I have no control of me
I’m just here watching it happen while the monster moves in my place
every word that comes out feels wrong, I know it shouldn’t be said but I can’t stop it
(Verse 2)
I can feel my hands move, I can hear my voice, I can see exactly what’s being done
But there’s this distance between me and it all, I’m there… but I’m not the one
The monster inside of me, it’s taking over slowly and I can’t do anything to stop it
I’m still here, I can see, but I have no control of me, I’m just forced to sit and watch it
I’m trapped behind my own eyes while something else decides what I do
I hear every word I speak, watch every action I take, im powerless to stop this nightmare
I see it in their faces every time it happens, right after something comes out wrong
And I’m already there knowing it shouldn’t have been said
But I’m stuck in the background of my own life, just watching it play out again
I remember all of it, every second, every word, every look that followed after
I don’t get to forget, I don’t get to separate, it stays with me like it’s mine to carry forever
(Chorus)
I’m not coming back, No… I can’t come back, I’m too far gone
The monster inside of me its taking over slowly
I’m still here, I can see, But I have no control of me
I’m just here watching it happen while the monster moves in my place
every word that comes out feels wrong, I know it shouldn’t be said but I can’t stop it
(Verse 3)
So I start pulling back, keeping distance, staying quiet when I can
Not because I want to… but because I know what happens if I don’t
I don’t trust what I’ll say before it comes out, I don’t trust what I’ll do
Because I know how fast it takes over, and there’s nothing I can do once it breaks through
I’m sitting here going over everything again, trying to catch it before it starts
But every time I think I’ve got it figured out, it just slips right past
And I know this won’t be the last time, I know how this ends
Me stuck here watching it happen… and then living through it again
(Bridge)
Don’t forgive me, If I hurt you
That wasn’t me… But it still came through
I was there for every second… just not the one in control
I felt every second, I watched every line
I knew what was happening, And still couldn’t fight
I kept trying to do something, anything… But nothing would respond
And it just kept going anyway, and I have to live through every second of it
(Final Chorus / Outro)
There’s no saving me now
I’m not coming back, No… I can’t come back, I’m too far gone
There’s no way to pretend
I’m not losing myself, I’m just losing control
The monster inside of me, it’s taking over slowly and I can’t come back, I’m too far gone
I’m trapped behind my own eyes while something else decides what I do
And the worst part of all of it… is I remember everything I saw

dropdowns.title: Beaten Down
dropdowns.about: Enduring years of bullying while refusing to break.
dropdowns.description: This track reflects a prolonged period of pressure and repeated hits, where the weight never really lets up. It carries the feeling of being pushed down, worn thin, and forced to hold ground with little relief. At its core, it’s about not giving in finding a way to stand back up, keep a voice, and turn that pressure into something that doesn’t break you.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
Yeah…
Still standing…
Verse 1
Pressure in my chest and it won’t let go
World keeps pushing but I won’t fold
Every step feels heavy, dragging slow
But I’m still here, yeah I still hold
Pre-Chorus
Weight on me, but I breathe it in
Tried to break me, didn’t cave me in
Every hit just pulls me thin
But I’m not done, I won’t give in
Chorus
I’m beaten down, but I’m not out
Still got a voice, still got a shout
Tried to bury me in the ground
But I rise up, I won't stay beaten down
Verse 2
Noise all around, yeah it cuts so deep
No place to rest, no time to sleep
Standing alone but I keep my feet
Even cracked, I don’t retreat
Pre-Chorus
Weight on me, but I breathe it in
Tried to break me, didn’t cave me in
Every hit just pulls me thin
But I’m not done, I won’t give in
Chorus
I’m beaten down, but I’m not out
Still got a voice, still got a shout
Tried to bury me in the ground
But I rise up, I won't stay beaten down
Verse 3
Hands on the ground but I’m pushing back
Every step forward, no fade to black
Weight still there but I carry that
Turn the pressure into impact
Pre-Chorus
Weight on me, but I breathe it in
Tried to break me, didn’t cave me in
Every hit just pulls me thin
But I’m not done, I won’t give in
Chorus
I’m beaten down, but I’m not out
Still got a voice, still got a shout
Tried to bury me in the ground
But I rise up, I won't stay beaten down
Bridge
Push, pull, breaking at the seams
Still holding onto what I believe
No light, no help, just me to be
The one who fights, the one who sees
Final Chorus
I’m beaten down, but I’m not out
Still got a voice, still got a shout
Tried to bury me in the ground
But I rise up, I won't stay beaten down
Outro
Beaten down… but I breathe again
Still standing here, never caving in
Every scar, every sound
Still rising up, i won’t stay beaten down
Beaten down… yeah I feel it now
But it won’t take me, won’t break me down
Through the noise, through the doubt
I’m still here and I’m breaking out

dropdowns.title: Anything But Me
dropdowns.about: Hiding who I am to avoid being targeted
dropdowns.description: This track reflects a period where being myself didn’t feel like an option. It’s about adapting to every room, reading people, and becoming whatever kept attention off me. Instead of pushing back, I learned to disappear—blending in, shutting down, and cutting off parts of myself before anyone else could. It’s survival through control, where staying unseen felt safer than being known

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
I’m restricted to be anybody but me
Every room decides who I should be
Read the air like classified code
Shift my voice to survival mode
“Hey, how you doing?” I’m good, I’m alright.
I say it so smooth you’d think I’m fine.
Rehearsed response, every time.
Sunglasses on so you can’t read my eyes
Static in one ear — drown the noise outside
Slip in quiet, slip out clean
Leave no trace I’ve ever been
Call me ghost, call me gone
Hard to break what’s not turned on
Pre-Chorus
I learned early — don’t react
Don’t give them something to attack
If I blur the edges of my name
No one gets close enough to aim
Chorus
I feel so restricted to be anything but me
Breathing low so no one sees
You can’t kill me if I’m already dead
I buried the heartbeat inside my head
Hollow chest and a porcelain grin
Bulletproof skin I’m trapped within
If I never let you underneath
Then I’m safe —
Restricted to be anybody but me
Verse 2
Secret agent in the crowd
Blend it down, don’t stand out loud
Every movement calculated slow
Disappear before it shows
I amputate what makes me real
Detach before I start to feel
Better numb than open wide
Better quiet than crucified
They only love what they can mold
So I trade my warmth for cold
File me down until I fit
Carve out everything that hits
Pre-Chorus
Don’t flinch. Don’t fight.
Don’t let them see the light.
If I erase enough of me
There’s nothing left to bleed.
Chorus (bigger)
I feel so restricted to be anything but me
Locked behind invisibility
You can’t break me if I don’t exist
I turned survival into this
Hollow ribs and vacant eyes
Living proof you can survive
But surviving isn’t relief
It’s just being —
Restricted to be anybody but me
Bridge (breakdown — heavy)
Go ahead — look right through me
I perfected invisibility
No pulse, no spark, no heat
Just echoes walking on two feet
I don’t fade — I phase out
Exit wound without a sound
I leave before the temperature turns
I don’t stay long enough to burn
Final Chorus (raw)
I feel so restricted to be anything but me
Even breathing feels like treachery
You can’t kill me — I’m already gone
Just skin and bone with armor on
Hollow chest and silent scream
I traded truth for in-between
If I never let you underneath
Then I’m safe —
Restricted to be anybody but me
Outro (soft, distant)
Call me ghost.
Call me gone.
Hard to hurt what isn’t on.
Still hollow.

dropdowns.title: Same Day Again
dropdowns.about: Stuck in the same cycle, day after day.
dropdowns.description: This track sits between losing myself and looking back on it. It reflects a stretch where everything blurred together—nothing changing, nothing improving, just repeating the same patterns over and over. Not as intense as what came before, but not resolved either, it carries the feeling of being stuck in motion without actually moving forward

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
Wake up late, same side of the bed
Same blank ceiling, same thoughts in my head
Phone lights up, I don’t check the name
Doesn’t matter — it’s all the same
tea cold but I drink it still
Just to say I had the will
Sit in silence, let time pass through
Nothing wrong, but nothing true
Pre-Chorus
No highs, no lows, no sharp, no sting
Just quiet where there should be something
I didn’t break, I didn’t bend
I just stopped somewhere in between
Chorus
Every day feels like the same day again
Same breath in, same empty chest
No crash, no burn, no need to pretend
Just nothing where I used to exist
Not alive, not dead, just set on repeat
No reason to run, no reason to leave
If this is safe, then why can’t I rest
Same day again —
Nothing left
Verse 2
Conversations blur into noise
Nods and smiles, automated poise
I say enough to pass the test
Never more, never less
Hours fade but they don’t move
Like I’m stuck in a waiting room
Watching life through muted glass
Let it happen, let it pass
Pre-Chorus
No fear left to even fight
No dark, no spark, no light
I didn’t win, I didn’t lose
I just got used to the view
Chorus (slightly bigger)
Every day feels like the same day again
Same steps traced in soft cement
No pain to prove I ever lived
No pulse to say what this is
Not alive, not dead, just in between
A quiet loop nobody sees
If this is peace, why does it press
Same day again —
Nothing left
Bridge (subtle shift — the first crack)
I don’t hurt… but I don’t feel healed
I don’t run… but I never deal
This isn’t war, this isn’t peace
It’s just a loop that doesn’t release
And maybe that’s what scares me most
Not the pain — but getting close
To staying here and calling it
Home
Final Chorus (connects forward into “Who I Was”)
Every day felt like the same day again
Till I noticed I never left
No chains, no guards, no one stepping in
Just me holding my own breath
Not alive, not dead, just incomplete
Standing still felt like defeat
If this is safe, then what’s it worth
Same day again —
Or something worse
Outro (soft, almost realization seed)
Nothing changed…
Because I didn’t.

dropdowns.title: Under the Tree
dropdowns.about: A song tied to my dad's death.
dropdowns.description: One of the most directly personal tracks on the album, this song centers on the loss of my dad. It marks a defining moment in my life and carries the weight, memory, and lasting emotional impact of that event.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
He was stubborn like the weather, never listened when it turned
Doctor said “slow down,” he just clocked in for another burn
Said he loved the house, but he was gone before the sun
Sat us in front of cartoons, then he disappeared to run
Always chasing down a sale like it meant something more
Fifteen of the same thing stacked behind a closet door
Hands in dirt, garden calling even when he couldn’t stand
Still out there breaking soil with a half-shaking hand
Took me out to shoot arrows every Saturday for years
Didn’t say a lot, but he kept showing up, he was there
Candy locked in trunks, in the fridge, in the car
Didn’t matter what he tried, I still found where they are
Chorus
And it didn’t hit like thunder, didn’t break me in two
Just a text on my phone in the middle of what I do
World didn’t stop spinning, didn’t fall off track
I just read it once, put it down… and went right back
And I know that sounds cold, but it’s honest and true
It wasn’t tears in the moment, just a quiet I grew into
Now when people ask where my dad might be
I just say “he’s sitting somewhere… under a tree.”
Verse 2
He could build a dream in sketches, every line was clean
Plans looked like a million bucks, better than you’ve seen
But somewhere past the start, when the point of no return hit
He’d step back, leave it breathing, never fully burn it
Walls half gone, floors half fixed, time just froze in place
Like the work and him were running in the same kind of race
But the few he finished? Damn, they shined like gold
Like he proved what he could be, then just never told
Tried to teach me on the road, I didn’t hear a thing
Lessons came too early, didn’t mean anything
Except one line he drilled into my head like a creed:
“If you’re early you’re on time, If you're on time you're late, if you're late you're fired.”
Chorus
And it didn’t hit like thunder, didn’t break me in two
Just a text on my phone in the middle of what I do
World didn’t stop spinning, didn’t fall off track
I just read it once, put it down… and went right back
And I know that sounds cold, but it’s honest and true
It wasn’t tears in the moment, just a quiet I grew into
Now when people ask where my dad might be
I just say “he’s sitting somewhere… under a tree.”
Verse 3
Hospitals got closer every couple of years
Like a clock counting down that nobody hears
Last year was a bed and a room and a wait
Like life was just stalling at the edge of a gate
He still talked like he’d leave, like he’d walk back home
Like the garden still needed him to turn that stone
But time doesn’t bargain, and it doesn’t explain
It just takes what it takes and leaves you the same
Didn’t cry that day, didn’t break that night
Didn’t feel a shift, didn’t feel it bite
Just another step in a line I was walking through
Like nothing really changed… even though it do
Chorus
And it didn’t hit like thunder, didn’t break me in two
Just a text on my phone in the middle of what I do
World didn’t stop spinning, didn’t fall off track
I just read it once, put it down… and went right back
And I know that sounds cold, but it’s honest and true
It wasn’t tears in the moment, just a quiet I grew into
Now when people ask where my dad might be
I just say “he’s sitting somewhere… under a tree.”
Outro
Couple months went by, then a couple more years
Still no flood coming in, still no breakdown tears
We just joke at the table like it’s easier that way
“My dad sits under a tree” — then we change what we say
People try to fix it, try to soften the line
But they don’t know the rhythm, don’t know it’s mine
And maybe one day it’ll hit, maybe it won’t
Maybe grief ain’t loud… maybe sometimes it just don’t
And I think about gardens he never got through
Half-built dreams, and the things he could do
And somewhere in all of it, messy but free
He’s finally got time… just sitting under a tree

dropdowns.title: Love Is Dead
dropdowns.about: Losing love and everything tied to it.
dropdowns.description: This track reflects a point where love didn’t just end, it collapsed completely, taking control, identity, and stability with it. It captures the feeling of giving everything to something that ultimately breaks, leaving nothing left to hold onto. In that aftermath, there’s both emptiness and a strange sense of release, where what was lost also removes what once held me in place.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: (Verse 1)
Love is dead, my love has died, and with it went my control
I felt it slipping piece by piece until there was nothing left to hold
Love is gone, I’ve lost it all, there’s nothing left to save
I tried to keep it breathing, but it buried me instead
(Chorus)
Love is dead, it’s gone, it’s over
There’s nothing left inside to hold on to
I can feel it fading colder
And there’s nothing left for me to lose
(Verse 2)
I can’t come back tonight, no… I’m not the same anymore
Something broke inside of me that I can’t ignore
I gave it everything I had, I let it take control
And when it died, it took with it every part of me I know
(Chorus)
Love is dead, it’s gone, it’s over
There’s nothing left inside to hold on to
I can feel it fading colder
And there’s nothing left for me to lose
(Bridge)
Death has come once again to set me free
Not from life… but from what love made of me
For love has killed me, left nothing behind
Just the silence where it used to be alive
(Outro)
Love has died and took the chains that bound me
And set me free
For love killed me
And there’s nothing left of me to be

dropdowns.title: Who I Was
dropdowns.about: Realizing I was the one holding myself back.
dropdowns.description: This track marks a shift in perspective, where everything I believed about what I was reacting to starts to change. It reflects the realization that the control, distance, and self-restriction I lived in weren’t just reactions to others, they were choices I continued to make. What once felt like defense starts to look like self-imposed limits, and the line between protection and confinement becomes clear. It’s not a breakdown or a breakthrough, just a quiet understanding that the barrier was never fully external.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
I don’t flinch anymore
I just adjust
Shift my weight
File down the rough
Speak in tones that don’t offend
Keep my volume under ten
Let the edges blur away
Safer that way
I map the exits when I walk in
Scan the eyes, predict the spin
Say enough to not seem cold
Never enough to unfold
Pre-Chorus
It’s not fear — it’s discipline
I don’t lose because I don’t begin
Can’t be cut if I don’t reach
Can’t be known if I don’t breach
Chorus (Shifted Meaning)
I was so restricted to be anything but me
But nobody was holding the key
I called it war, I called it threat
But most were fighting themselves instead
Hollow wasn’t what they made
It was the shelter that I stayed
If I never let me breathe
Then I’m the one —
Restricting me
SHIFT
(No anger. No breakdown. Just realization.)
Verse 2 (Life Change Perspective)
But something changed — not loud, not fast
Just the weight of moments stacking past
Rooms got bigger, voices small
No one watching me at all
Turns out people disappear
Not from spite — just out of fear
They guard themselves the way I did
Armor doesn’t stay a kid
The world kept moving without my mask
Didn’t notice the act I cast
All that effort just to hide
From people hiding just like I
Pre-Chorus 2
It wasn’t bullets, it was time
It wasn’t cruelty, just the climb
I kept shrinking for a crowd
That never looked that closely down
Chorus (Shifted Meaning)
I was so restricted to be anything but me
But nobody was holding the key
I called it war, I called it threat
But most were fighting themselves instead
Hollow wasn’t what they made
It was the shelter that I stayed
If I never let me breathe
Then I’m the one —
Restricting me
Bridge (heavy, grounded)
You grow up and things get real
Loss hits harder than a feel
The rooms don’t care, the lights don’t chase
The mirror doesn’t change your face
I kept ghosting out of sight
But life doesn’t stop to fight
It just keeps moving whether you
Are hollow — or breaking through
Final Chorus (Acceptance — not triumph)
I was restricted to be anything but me
But that restriction came from me
You couldn’t kill what wasn’t shown
I buried truth in undertone
Hollow wasn’t armor plated
It was fear I cultivated
If I never let me breathe
Then I’m the one —
Who buried me
Outro (soft, sober)
Anything but me…
Was easier.
Until it wasn’t.

dropdowns.title: Free To Be Me
dropdowns.about: Stepping into who I am without apology.
dropdowns.description: This track closes on identity, freedom, and self-acceptance. After everything before it, this song represents reaching a place where I can stand as myself more fully, without hiding, bending, or being defined only by what happened to me.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
Yeah, right now this is exactly who I am
Verse 1
I walk in, take a second just to read the room
I don’t rush, I observe, I decide my move
When I know what I’m dealing with I take control

Everything I am is something I control
Chorus

Final Chorus
I’m free to dress how I want, I don’t follow a line
I’m free to do it my way every step, every time
No voice telling me who I should try to be

Yeah, this is who I am and I’m not changing it

sections.title: EZEOEZED LDEZLO
sections.about: An affirmation-driven album designed to reinforce confidence, direction, and personal growth throughout the year.
body: EZEOEZED LDEZLO was created as a musical version of my daily affirmations for 2026. Instead of repeating them every morning and evening, I transformed them into songs that can be played and sung along with throughout the day.
Each track focuses on a specific mindset or life area, helping reinforce positive direction while also capturing the major transitions happening during this period of my life.

dropdowns.title: Grounded Fire
dropdowns.about: A song built from affirmations focused on confidence and boldness.
dropdowns.description: Grounded Fire is designed to reinforce both stability and courage. The song emphasizes remaining grounded while still having the confidence to move forward and take decisive action.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
I command respect without demanding a thing
Calm in my presence, feel the strength that I bring.
Grounded and steady, I don’t shake, I don’t bend
I’m fully myself from the start to the end.
I move with control, but my energy’s warm
Quiet confidence, steady in the storm.
I lead with truth, with a calm authority
I radiate power just by being me.
Pre-Chorus
Every step I take, I know my worth
Every breath I claim, I trust my nerve.
I don’t chase, I align, I let it flow
What’s meant for me already knows.
Chorus
I’m bold, I take initiative, I trust my way
Fearless heart, I don’t hesitate.
Magnetic, confident, clear and free
Everything I am comes naturally.
I move with courage, I stand in my light
Calm authority, grounded fire inside.
Verse 2
I’m charming, expressive, confident and clean
Owning my energy, seen without being seen.
Strength and warmth in the same damn frame
I don’t force attraction—it knows my name.
I handle moments with confidence and grace
Playful wit, steady eyes, easy pace.
Every interaction feels light, feels true
People lean in just to feel what I do.
Pre-Chorus 2
I know my value, I walk it loud
Not with noise—but presence now.
I focus on growth, I invest in me
Every day stronger than yesterday was.
Chorus
I’m bold, I take initiative, I trust my way
Fearless heart, I don’t hesitate.
Magnetic, confident, clear and free
Everything I am comes naturally.
I radiate charm, warmth, and truth
Confidence lives in everything I do.
Bridge (Caitlin – Energy & Alignment)
Our interactions spark joy and ease
No pressure, no force—just energy.
She feels my presence, I feel her light
Every exchange leaves us smiling inside.
Aligned with the love we both recognize
Magnetic moments, no need to try.
I radiate what she resonates with
Playful confidence, calm, authentic.
Final Chorus
I attract respect, admiration, connection
Meaningful moments, natural direction.
I’m confident, desirable, fully aware
Of the man I am and the energy I share.
I trust myself, I trust the flow
What’s aligned with me continues to grow.
Grounded, bold, calm fire inside—
I move through the world with clarity and pride.
Outro
I don’t force outcomes, I let them arrive
I lead with presence—that’s how I thrive.
Strong, magnetic, fully me…
Everything I want comes effortlessly.

dropdowns.title: The Perfect One For Me
dropdowns.about: A reflection on the qualities I hope to find in a romantic partner.
dropdowns.description: This song was created from a personal list describing the kind of partner I would like to build a life with. While it describes an ideal, the song also acknowledges that real people will never perfectly match a list. One lyric intentionally adds humor to the idea: “She can cook, she can clean, and she can playfully get me to do it for her.”

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
I’m not asking for perfection
just someone who feels like home.
And every time I picture that
I see the kind of woman I’ve always known—
Verse 1
She takes care of herself
got that glow you don’t fake.
Well-groomed, kind-hearted
all the real things people break.
Motivated with direction
knows exactly what she wants—
and she’ll put the work behind it
’cause excuses never haunt.
Pre-Chorus
She’s got a mind that matches mine
same rhythm in the way we think.
A little passive, little bold—
moves steady, never on the brink.
Chorus
That’s the perfect one for me—
the kind who brings out something bright.
Loves good music, laughs easily
keeps her standards set just right.
She can cook, she can clean
and she can playfully get me to do it for her.
Yeah, that’s the woman I can see—
the perfect one for me.
Verse 2
Spends her free time lifting spirits
works out, plays games, unwinds.
Likes a show on in the background
’cause peace is hard to find.
Caring without smothering
soft touch, quality time—
and the way she moves through life
just fits the rhythm of mine.
Pre-Chorus
No need for crowds to tell her who she is
she walks her own line clean.
She’s sweet but she’s protective
of the places she’s unseen.
Chorus
That’s the perfect one for me—
the kind who brings out something bright.
Loves good music, laughs easily
keeps her standards set just right.
She can cook, she can clean
and she can playfully get me to do it for her.
Yeah, that’s the woman I can see—
the perfect one for me.
Bridge
She doesn’t need to be flawless
she just needs to be real with me.
Someone I can match my energy to—
easy, warm, and free.
If she’s out there on her own path
we’ll cross when life agrees.
Outro
I’m not chasing some illusion
I’m choosing who I want to be.
And when the right one stands beside me—
I’ll know instantly…
she’s the perfect one for me.

dropdowns.title: More Than Enough Time

Verse 3 – Free Time

dropdowns.title: Already In Changing Motion
dropdowns.about: A reflection on a year filled with overlapping life transitions.
dropdowns.description: This song was written about a period where multiple major life changes were happening at once. School, work, volunteering, business projects, and searching for a home all began moving forward simultaneously, creating the feeling of constant momentum.

Under everything I do

Making room for something new
Chorus

Verse 2

Breath comes easy in my chest

Chorus
This year’s big changes will flow and go smoothly

Already in changing motion
Bridge

Without me chasing what they’ll be
I’m not standing at a beginning
I’m not waiting at an end
I’m in the quiet middle movement
Where everything starts to bend
Soft Break
And it feels…
Like exhaling after holding too long
Like waking up inside a better season
Final Chorus
This year’s big changes will flow and go smoothly
I don’t have to make them arrive
Every shift is kind, every turn is gentle

No collision, no confusion

Life is unfolding without me pushing
Already in changing motion
Outro

Already forming
Already becoming something new
Already moving
Already forming
Already in changing motion

sections.title: IEIIZS
body: IEIIZS is a narrative album built around a series of songs that were rewritten and arranged to tell a connected story. While many of the pieces were originally written independently, the first three songs created the foundation for a larger storyline that the rest of the album was adapted to support.
The album explores relationships, memory, personal growth, and the internal struggles that shape how we see ourselves and others.

dropdowns.title: What Time Stole Away
dropdowns.about: A transitional piece connecting the search in "Decade Search" to the events of "Second Meeting."
dropdowns.description: This song acts as a bridge within the album's storyline, reflecting on how time changes memories and expectations. It captures the how time can make memories blurry or just fade away.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
I don’t recall the whole of youth
those early days have slipped away—
but I remember saying your name
in that homeschool hallway.
A folded note, a quiet “yes,”
your sister’s voice that tried to sway…
but your shy smile stayed in my chest
when all else washed away.
Chorus
I don’t remember everything
but something in me stayed—
the spark we shared in two-thousand-six
despite what time stole away.
The memories blur and fade to dust
the details lost and gray—
but somehow you remained the part
time never stole away.
Verse 2
I barely hold those scattered days
just flashes in the gray and gold—
a party chair, you leaned on me
the moment you opened up to me.
Your long brunette hair, the quiet laugh
still echo from that day—
the rest is gone to distant years
but that time couldn’t take.
Outro
So here I stand with fragments left
too few for me to say—
yet still enough to light the path
despite what time stole away.

dropdowns.title: Decade Search
dropdowns.about: A narrative song based on journal entries documenting a long search for a childhood friend.
dropdowns.description: In 2015 I began searching for a childhood friend I had lost contact with. Over the years I wrote journal entries documenting the search and the thoughts that came with it. This song adapts those entries into a condensed narrative, shaping years of reflection into a single story-driven track.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
Across the silence, across the years
Through endless hope, and quiet tears
Seasons have turned, the clocks have spun
Yet still I chase where it all begun
Through every turn, through every bend
Seventeen years, and still I know
This is the story I won’t let end
Chorus A
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
I promised once, I promise still
No matter how long the nights or days
Somehow, someday, I will cross your way
Verse 1
Back in two-thousand-eight, you moved away
And the purple letters on a dark screen faded away
And then I wrote myself the one to fight and defend
And you, the love I’d chase until the end
Though time has stolen what still remained
The signed photo of you is now gone from view
Yet the picture in my heart shines brighter every day
Chorus A
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
I promised once, I promise still
No matter how long the nights or days
Somehow, someday, I will cross your way
Verse 2
I was looking at projects when I saw your name
That year I was there, I spent it looking for thee
I can never forget, your name etched in my soul
Each echo I hear, my eyes search the mist for you
Chorus A
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
I promised once, I promise still
No matter how long the nights or days
Somehow, someday, I will cross your way
Verse 3
At eighteen I opened the world online
The very next day, I searched for your sign
Social media gave hope to find, but easy not would it be
Through countless names, still none belonged to thee
Like chasing true gold where only fools gold lies
Yet year after year, I’d try once more
Calling your name through a million doors
Chorus A
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
I promised once, I promise still
No matter how long the nights or days
Somehow, someday, I will cross your way
Verse 4
I turned to friends who search the shadows
To trace your name through time and space
But with only whispers, what remained were echoes fading
Though the road is lost and endless
One day, somehow, I’ll find my way back to you
Chorus A
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
I promised once, I promise still
No matter how long the nights or days
Somehow, someday, I will cross your way
Verse 5
December twenty-twenty-four I searched through faded photographs
Hoping your smile would meet my eyes, yet your face remained unseen.
The night I held my mother’s hard-drive, a vault of memories
Photographs emerged, like stars against the night
A single step closer, the farthest I’ve come
In a decade of restless searching
Excitement surged, then broke like tides
Rising, falling, too wild to contain
Chorus B
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
Through endless roads, and force of will
No matter how far, no matter how long
Every road I walk leads me back to you
Verse 6
Seventeen years later, a decade of searching
There in the archives I glimpsed your name
My heart surged, overflowing, could this truly be?
Emotions tumbled like rivers, racing over every thought
Tender and torn, like a storm
So I took up my pen, words pouring out like rivers unbound
On stationery I traced every letter
Six hours of cursive, each stroke for you
Sealed in an envelope, wax pressed with care
I sent my heart through paper and ink
Hoping the universe will deliver it to you
That I might hear the echo of your reply
Chorus B
Across the years, across the miles
Through fading screens and endless trials
Through endless roads, and force of will
No matter how far, no matter how long
Every road I walk leads me back to you
Verse 7
When your message appeared, my whole system stalled
The words wouldn’t come, though my heart recalled
My brain kept crashing it could not reboot
Across the years, across the miles
My soul will always come back to you
Outro
Across the years, across the miles
Through endless roads and countless trials
The years may pass and miles extend
Still every road will find its end
And when it does, I know it’s true
The end of the road will lead to you

dropdowns.title: Second Meeting
dropdowns.about: A song describing a real encounter that followed reconnecting with someone.
dropdowns.description: After reconnecting with a friend through conversation, we eventually met for coffee. This song captures the mix of familiarity and uncertainty that can come from meeting someone again after time apart. This is the second version of the song. The revision involved a small lyrical change to better match how the moment was remembered — how the same eyes could appear different depending on the light.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: intro
We met back in two-thousand-six
A shy hello, a folded note, a gentle twist.
At your birthday party, side by side
You came out of your shell, just a bit.
Seventeen years, the world spun round
But the memory stayed, it never drowned.
Verse 1
I saw you walk in, didn’t know it was you
Still as gorgeous as the day I once knew.
The whole room faded, my eyes couldn’t stray
You stood out in the most beautiful way.
Chorus
On a Saturday in June I had coffee with you
Meeting you again felt like something brand new
Seventeen years vanished, my heart shining through
You’re still as breathtaking as the day I first knew.
Verse 2
You asked if I was the one you came for
My heart froze, I couldn’t think anymore.
The face I had noticed was the face from my past
Seventeen years gone, but the moment still lasts.
Chorus
On a Saturday in June I had coffee with you
Meeting you again felt like something brand new
Seventeen years vanished, my heart shining through
You’re still as breathtaking as the day I first knew.
Verse 3
Black hair, tattoos, a scar on your lip
But jade green eyes I could never forget.
I saw you look down when I caught your gaze
But your quiet smile still lights up the place.
Chorus
On a Saturday in June I had coffee with you
Meeting you again felt like something brand new
Seventeen years vanished, my heart shining through
You’re still as breathtaking as the day I first knew.
Verse 4
Since that coffee, something’s changed in me
More than I thought it was meant to be.
I can’t go back to “just reconnect,”
The truth is stronger than I expected.
Outro
I love the way your laughter pulls me near
The way you shine makes the moment clear.
It’s more than casual, more than a friend
I want to see where this road could bend.

dropdowns.title: The Unreachable Flower
dropdowns.about: A reflection on longing for something meaningful that may always remain out of reach.
dropdowns.description: This song explores the emotional tension between hope and acceptance. It focuses on pursuing something beautiful or meaningful while recognizing that it may never fully be attained.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro 70 BPM
Verse 1
I saw you bloom beneath the sky
A fleeting color that caught my eye.
I reached my hand, but the breeze pulled away
The petals danced where I could not stay.
Chorus (stronger dynamics)
You’re the flower just beyond my hand
Rooted deep in another land.
I climb, I fall, but still I try
To touch the beauty that passes by.
Forever close, forever far
A missed chance shining like a star.
Verse 2 (soft again)
I’ve crossed the thorns, I’ve faced the rain
Each attempt ends in the same refrain.
The closer I get, the farther you seem
Like chasing sunlight inside a dream.
Chorus (stronger dynamics)
You’re the flower just beyond my hand
Rooted deep in another land.
I climb, I fall, but still I try
To touch the beauty that passes by.
Forever close, forever far
A missed chance shining like a star.
Verse 3 (rising emotion)
Seasons change but I don’t let go
Your fragrance lingers, it tells me so.
Though I may never hold you near
The chase itself keeps my heart clear.
Outro (slow down rift, soft fade)
You’re the flower I cannot claim
Yet I reach for you all the same.
A gentle ghost, a fleeting song
The love I’ve chased my whole life long.

dropdowns.title: Fortress Of My Mind
dropdowns.about: A song about emotional defenses built from past experiences.
dropdowns.description: After being hurt in the past, I built emotional walls to avoid repeating those experiences. This song tells the story of someone who managed to break through those defenses for a short time before the relationship came to an end.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: (Verse 1)
She marched through my daydreams with banners held high
Trumpets and laughter, a gleam in her eye
My heart was a citadel built stone by stone
But her smile found the cracks and claimed it her own.
(Pre-Chorus)
I swore I’d defend it, I swore I was strong
But love turns to rhythm, and reason’s all wrong—
(Chorus)
Oh she’s dancing inside
Through the fortress of my mind!
Every thought she conquers, every wall she climbs
I can’t hold the line this time!
She’s the banner unfurled
The storm that’s taken my world
She’s the queen of the chaos I find—
In the fortress of my mind!
(Verse 2)
I patched up the gateways with silence and pride
But laughter came seeping from cracks I’d denied
She painted the stonework in colors so wild
And tore down my armor with one fleeting smile.
(Chorus)
Oh she’s dancing inside
Through the fortress of my mind!
Every thought she conquers, every wall she climbs
I can’t hold the line this time!
She’s the banner unfurled
The storm that’s taken my world
She’s the queen of the chaos I find—
In the fortress of my mind!
(Verse 3)
Now cannons are violins, fire’s turned to song
The war drums are spinning the dancers along
She reigns with no crown, just a touch so kind
That peace feels like battle inside of my mind.
(Chorus)
Oh she’s dancing inside
Through the fortress of my mind!
Every thought she conquers, every wall she climbs
I can’t hold the line this time!
She’s the banner unfurled
The storm that’s taken my world
She’s the queen of the chaos I find—
In the fortress of my mind!
(Bridge)
Let the flags fly tattered and torn
I’ve never felt freer in the storm
If surrender’s a sin, then let judgment be blind—
For I’m lost, and I’m glad, in the fortress of my mind!
(Final Chorus)
Oh she’s dancing inside
Through the fortress of my mind!
Every thought she conquers, every wall she climbs
I can’t hold the line this time!
She’s the banner unfurled
The storm that’s taken my world
And I’m smiling at what she’s designed—
In the fortress of my mind!
(Playful Outro)
So sound the retreat, but don’t be surprised
If I leave all the gates open wide—
After all, it’s divine to be captured in kind
In the fortress… of my mind!

dropdowns.title: I Can't Get You Out Of My Head
dropdowns.about: A song about the persistence of attraction and lingering thoughts.
dropdowns.description: This track describes the experience of having someone constantly on your mind no matter how much you try to focus on other things. The repetition of those thoughts becomes both frustrating and strangely compelling.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
I think I’m going crazy, I can’t get you out of my head.
When I think of you, my heart starts to ache
Feels like it might break.
But when I’m around you, the butterflies start flying
And suddenly I don’t know what to say.
Chorus
You are always on my mind, night and day you’re on my mind.
I don’t know, I don’t know how to get you out of my head.
Oh why, why, why can’t I get you out of my head?
Oh why, tell me why I can’t get you out of my head.
Verse 2
I think I’m going crazy, you’re always stuck in my mind.
At night I can’t sleep, and I don’t know why—hi—
But when I do, I’m dreaming of you.
I’ve got so many thoughts I wanna tell you
But when I try to speak, I get so nervous I could die—hi—
Chorus
You are always on my mind, night and day you’re on my mind.
I don’t know, I don’t know how to get you out of my head.
Oh why, why, why can’t I get you out of my head?
Oh why, tell me why I can’t get you out of my head.
Verse 3
I think I’m going crazy, I can’t get you out of my head.
Your beautiful smile would be enough for me
But when you smile at me, I think I lose my head.
Guitar Riff
Chorus
You are always on my mind, night and day you’re on my mind.
I don’t know, I don’t know how to get you out of my head.
Oh why, why, why can’t I get you out of my head?
Oh why, tell me why I can’t get you out of my head.
Outro
Oh why, oh why does it have to be this way… (carry out)

dropdowns.title: Two Months And A Message
dropdowns.about: A reflection on communicating with someone who replies on their own timeline.
dropdowns.description: This song was inspired by a friend who sometimes takes a long time to respond to messages. However, when they do reply, their responses are thoughtful and detailed. The song balances the frustration of waiting with appreciation for the depth of those conversations.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Verse 1
Two months went quiet, and I kept checking every night
Staring at the screen like maybe I just missed the light
And every day I’d wonder if I said the wrong thing
Replay every moment until I couldn’t breathe
My mind kept running circles, tightening the spin
Telling me she’s gone again, telling me “this is the end”
But somewhere deep inside, a softer voice still stayed
Whispering “just hold on… maybe she’s just delayed”
Pre-Chorus
My sister said “you’re wasting time, she’s not coming back”
Those words sank heavy, almost knocked me off track
But my brother told me “man, you gotta stay strong
Give it some space, don’t assume something’s wrong”
And my friend said “reach out once more, stay kind
Not everyone disappears just to hurt your mind”
Chorus
So I held on
Even when the silence felt like a storm
Even when my doubts tried to change my form
I kept a little hope alive inside my chest
Praying someday we’d talk again, and this wouldn’t be the end
I held on
Through the fear, through the noise, through the endless ache
Waiting for the moment something finally breaks
And then it came…
A message with my name
Verse 2
You said “I’m so sorry for completely disappearing”
Said work was drowning you, the pressure kept interfering
Said you didn’t mean to hurt me or make me wait
Life got overwhelming, and you just broke a little late
And suddenly the weight I’d carried every day
Started melting like the fear was never here to stay
Your words were soft, real, and honest in their tone
And all that quiet time didn’t feel so cold alone
Bridge
Maybe the lesson hiding in that heavy pause
Was learning not to tear myself apart without a cause
Not every silence means goodbye, or something’s wrong
Sometimes people just need space to carry on
And I’m glad I kept the hope, glad I stayed
Glad I listened to the ones who told me not to fade
Because hearing from you
Felt like sunlight breaking through
Final Chorus
So I held on
Even when the silence felt like a storm
Even when my doubts tried to change my form
And now your message washed the shadows off my chest
And I finally breathed again, knowing this wasn’t the end
I held on
Through the fear, through the noise, through the endless ache
And when you came back, everything finally changed
You said my name…
And I knew you still cared the same

dropdowns.title: Knight In Shining Armor
dropdowns.about: A rewritten version of one of the earliest songs I wrote.
dropdowns.description: This song was originally written when I was younger and far more idealistic. Revisiting it years later felt both nostalgic and slightly embarrassing, but instead of discarding it I rewrote and expanded the song while preserving the original core theme.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
In the stillness of the twilight hour
When moonlight crowns the quiet fields
Know that my oath is sworn to thee
And all my heart before thee yields
Verse 1
Anytime you need, I’ll be right there
Ready to be your knight, to protect and defend, when you call my name
Ready to go through hell and endure any trial for you
To walk with you through the weary roads you walk
Chorus
I’d be happy to do anything you ask me to
As thou art a princess fair to mine
And I’ll be the knight in shining armor
Who stands ready to shield you from your troubles
Verse 2
Through forests deep and mountains high
Where ancient winds in whispers speak
I’ll stand beside thee steadfastly
A shield when thou art faint or weak.
For every fear that stirs thy soul
I’ll face the storm so thou needst not—
No shadow born of fate or night
Shall claim the joy that thou hast sought.
Chorus
I’d be happy to do anything you ask me to
As thou art a princess fair to mine
And I’ll be the knight in shining armor
Who stands ready to shield you from your troubles
Verse 3
And should the road grow dark and long
Or kingdoms rise and fall away
My vow remains unbroken still
To guard thee come what trials may.
For in my breast thy light resides
A beacon bright through every test—
And I shall roam a thousand realms
If it leads thee unto peace and rest.
Outro
All you have to do is ask
And I’ll help you bear the weight you shouldn’t carry alone
So Share with me the troubles heavy on your heart
And you can live happily ever after.
Yes, you can live happily ever after.

dropdowns.title: Wild And Free
dropdowns.about: A reflection on a restless and constantly wandering mind.
dropdowns.description: This song was written during a time when focusing on a single idea felt nearly impossible. My thoughts constantly jumped between new plans and possibilities until I eventually learned to embrace that creative wandering instead of resisting it.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
Sometimes my thoughts get tangled up
Like they’re hiding parts of me—
Wish I could catch them for a moment
Before they slip and run free.
Verse 1
I feel so restricted to be anything but me
Yet my mind runs wild and free—
And that’s the way it’s meant to be
Always wild, always free
Always running away from me.
Chorus
I must let it free
And hope it finds its way back to me—
My mind still runs wild and free
And that’s the way it’s meant to be
Forever wild, forever free
Always running away from me.
Verse 2
I tried talking to you
But my brain packed up its luggage
Took every bit of knowledge
And ran, ran right away from me—
And now I’ve got no idea what to say.
Chorus
I must let it free
And hope it finds its way back to me—
My mind still runs wild and free
And that’s the way it’s meant to be
Forever wild, forever free
Always running away from me.
Bridge
Maybe one day I’ll understand
Why my mind won’t stay in my hands—
Why it drifts, why it slips
Why it leaves me standing here like this.
But if it wanders far tonight
I’ll leave a light so it knows its way
Back to me someday.
Outro
So go on, run wild if you need—
I won’t force you to stay with me.
But when you’re tired of drifting free
Come home, come back
Return to me.
And maybe then I’ll finally see
The part of me that’s been running free.

dropdowns.title: The Quiet Vow
dropdowns.about: A song written in the style of traditional sword dance music.
dropdowns.description: I enjoy historical and medieval dances, especially sword dances. Because there are relatively few modern songs designed for them, I wrote this piece to fill that gap while still fitting within the narrative of the album.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
Before the steel, before the flame
Before the years could shift my name
There was a path I once passed through—
And still it draws me back to you.
Verse 0
The dawn breaks faint on fields grown old
On tales once whispered, never told.
Yet in the hush of morning’s hue
I find the thread that leads to you.
Chorus
Turn the blade, turn the fate
Keep the vow that time shall not break.
In the circle, hearts hold true
Every step returns to you.
Verse 1
I walk the path I knew before
Where once my younger heart did swear.
Some faces fade, as others do
Yet one remains, and draws me through.
Chorus
Turn the blade, turn the fate
Keep the vow that time shall not break.
In the circle, hearts hold true
Every step returns to you.
Verse 2
The years pass by like drifting flame
Yet something steadfast still remains.
Not loud, nor lost, nor rendered new—
But quiet strength I learned of you.
Chorus
Turn the blade, turn the fate
Keep the vow that time shall not break.
In the circle, hearts hold true
Every step returns to you.
Bridge
Though many roads before me lie
And tempests rise beneath the sky
One guiding thought still carries through—
What once I held, I hold for you.
Bridge
Turn the blade, turn the fate
Keep the vow that time shall not break.
In the circle, hearts hold true
Every step returns to you.
Bridge
I speak it not for all to hear;
It rises neither from doubt nor fear.
But still I bear it, firm and true—
My steps are mine, yet shaped by you.
Final Chorus
Turn the blade, turn the fate
Keep the vow that time shall not break.
In the circle, hearts hold true
Every step returns to you.
Outro
So on I walk the path I knew
Still holding fast, still moving through.
The dance goes on, the circle too—
And every step returns to you.

dropdowns.title: The Parts You Don't See
dropdowns.about: A personal reflection on the deeper aspects of identity.
dropdowns.description: People often ask how to describe yourself, but most introductions only capture surface details. This song explores the deeper motivations, experiences, and perspectives that shape who I really am.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro - The Quiet Part (Nordic mist + forest sounds)
In the hush of ancient forests
where the moonlight threads the leaves
I walk the paths inside my mind
that no one ever sees.
A stillness holds my spirit close
a whisper soft and deep-
the place where all my hidden parts
still wander in their sleep.
Chorus (emotional fantasy-lift (big reverb vocals, echo, light drums))
These are the parts you never see
the hidden realms inside of me-
the quiet voice, the loyal flame
the love I guard without a name.
If ever you looked close enough
perhaps you’d glimpse the whole of me-
the tangle of my heart and soul
the parts I fear you’ll never see
Verse 1 - The Shy Part (soft medieval fantasy with gentle flute/pad)
I am the voice behind the silence
the words I never dare to speak-
my courage stirs in trembling breaths
that fade before they reach.
I stand in shadows, soft and watching
hoping you might somehow know
that underneath this quiet shell
a gentler heartbeat grows.
Chorus (emotional fantasy-lift (big reverb vocals, echo, light drums))
These are the parts you never see
the hidden realms inside of me-
the quiet voice, the loyal flame
the love I guard without a name.
If ever you looked close enough
perhaps you’d glimpse the whole of me-
the tangle of my heart and soul
the parts I fear you’ll never see
Verse 2 - The Loyal Protector (soft war drums + low strings (not battle, more heartbeat rhythm))
Yet in my chest, a fire burns-
a steadfast, loyal, guarding flame.
I’ve walked through storms beside the ones
whose hearts I swore to claim.
Not warrior’s pride, nor armored boast
but the vow I keep unseen:
to shield the ones I hold most dear
like a knight for his queen.
Chorus (emotional fantasy-lift (big reverb vocals, echo, light drums))
These are the parts you never see
the hidden realms inside of me-
the quiet voice, the loyal flame
the love I guard without a name.
If ever you looked close enough
perhaps you’d glimpse the whole of me-
the tangle of my heart and soul
the parts I fear you’ll never see
Verse 3 - The Romantic & Artistic Soul (Chinese-ballad emotional softness + subtle fantasy elements)
A thousand colors bloom within
painted skies of dusk and dawn.
I love too deep, I feel too far
and hide it when you’re gone.
Songs and dreams soft-woven through
the forests of my heart-
the kind of love that lives in myths
yet breaks me to impart.
Chorus (emotional fantasy-lift (big reverb vocals, echo, light drums))
These are the parts you never see
the hidden realms inside of me-
the quiet voice, the loyal flame
the love I guard without a name.
If ever you looked close enough
perhaps you’d glimpse the whole of me-
the tangle of my heart and soul
the parts I fear you’ll never see
Verse 4 - The Coder / Thinker / World-Builder (subtle electronic/ambient layers under medieval instrumentation)
My mind builds worlds of starlit stone
of code and craft entwined-
a castle formed of logic’s fire
and stories undefined.
Patterns bloom like lantern light
in chambers of my mind-
a maze of thoughts both sharp and soft
the truth I hope you’ll find.
Bridge - If You Saw Me Truly (mixture of all the genres - the hybrid moment)
If you walked these halls within me
past the shadows, past the fear
you’d find a world of open skies
that longs to draw you near.
Not perfect, no-yet honest still
a spirit wild and free…
I wonder if you’d understand
the man I’ve always been to be.
Outro Returning to the Quiet Self (back to the deep quiet spiritual (similar to intro))
So back I drift through forest paths
to where my secrets softly lie-
until the day you chance to see
the truth I keep inside.
And if you never see it all
these parts remain in harmony-
the shy, the brave, the heart, the mind-
the parts you’ll someday see in me.

dropdowns.title: Delightfully Late Responder
dropdowns.about: A lighthearted reflection on a friend who takes a long time to reply to messages.
dropdowns.description: This song was written about a friend who often takes weeks or months to respond to messages. Despite the delays, their replies are thoughtful and detailed, often arriving as long multi-paragraph responses. Rather than seeing them as a bad texter, I prefer to think of them as a delightfully late responder.

dropdowns.title: Lyrics
dropdowns: Intro
You said, “Sorry for the delay—
must’ve missed the notification.”
And I laughed because that’s so you,
your little self-proclamation.
“Horrible texter,” that’s what you wrote,
but honestly, I never saw it that way—
your messages feel like postcards
arriving fashionably late.
Verse 1
You said you’re not on your phone a lot,
and texting’s never been your thing,
that messages slip past you
like birds forgetting how to sing.
But every time your name pops up,
it brightens up my day—
your words may wander through the clouds,
but they always find their way.
Chorus
So take your time, I don’t mind,
I’ll read your words whenever they arrive.
You call it slow, but I call it sweet—
little echoes drifting back to me.
You say you’re bad at texting,
but that’s not what I see—
every message you send feels worth the wait,
’cause they come from you to me.
Verse 2
I joked about it playfully,
but never meant to tease your heart—
your messages were thoughtful,
never rushed, never torn apart.
Three or four days never felt long,
not once did I complain—
’cause you reply like morning sun:
slow to rise, but worth the wait again.
Chorus
So take your time, I don’t mind,
I’ll read your words whenever they arrive.
You call it slow, but I call it sweet—
little echoes drifting back to me.
You say you’re bad at texting,
but that’s not what I see—
every message you send feels worth the wait,
’cause they come from you to me.
Verse 3
You worry you’re a bad texter,
but to me that’s far from true—
you’re a “delightfully late responder,”
and honestly, that suits you.
So take your time, no pressure here,
there’s no rush, no clock to beat—
your messages land right on their time,
and they always feel complete.
Chorus
So take your time, I don’t mind,
I’ll read your words whenever they arrive.
You call it slow, but I call it sweet—
little echoes drifting back to me.
You say you’re bad at texting,
but that’s not what I see—
every message you send feels worth the wait,
’cause they come from you to me.
Outro
So if you drift between replies,
just know I’m totally okay—
your words arrive like little stars
that brighten up my day.
No need to hurry, no need to stress,
just message when you’re free—
I’ll always smile at what you send,
whenever it wanders back to me.
